data.title: What's Going On With SoFi Technologies Stock Thursday?
data.description:
The Nasdaq is down 0.18% while the S&P 500 has shed 0.17%.

Investor positioning ahead of SoFi’s first-quarter results on April 29 is also driving near-term volatility.

At the same time, escalating geopolitical tensions have dampened risk appetite.

SoFi Launches Digital HELOC

Separately, SoFi on Wednesday announced an expansion of its home lending business with a fully digital HELOC platform and a new Real Estate Advisory Council, aiming to simplify access to home equity and improve the homebuying experience.

The move targets rising demand for home equity financing, as more homeowners stay put and tap existing property value. SoFi said it serves over 135,000 homeowners, with originations nearly doubling in 2025.

The company is also scaling its loan officer network and agent partnerships to speed up transactions and offer competitive rates, flexible terms, and added incentives like lender credits.

Technical Analysis

SoFi is sitting in the middle of its 52-week range ($10.80 to $32.73), which fits a “reset” phase after last year’s run and subsequent pullback. The stock is trading 10.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 14.7% below its 100-day SMA, a split that suggests short-term strength but a still-damaged intermediate trend.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is above its signal line and the histogram is positive, which leans toward improving upside momentum versus the prior downtrend. In everyday terms, MACD being above the signal line points to buyers gaining traction, even if the longer trend hasn’t fully healed.

The death cross in March (50-day SMA below the 200-day SMA) still matters because it often acts like “overhead gravity” during rebounds. That’s reinforced by the stock trading 21.2% below its 200-day SMA, which is consistent with sellers defending longer-term levels on strength.

  • Key Resistance: $19.00 — a nearby area where rebounds can stall if sellers stay active.
  • Key Support: $16.50 — a level where buyers previously showed up and may defend again.

SoFi is up 59.88% over the last 12 months, which shows the longer-term tape has still rewarded dip-buying despite big swings. The catch is that the stock remains well below its 52-week high, so bulls typically want to see sustained reclaiming of longer-term averages to confirm a cleaner trend.

SoFi Earnings & Analyst Outlook

The countdown is on: SoFi Technologies is set to report earnings on April 29, 2026 (confirmed).

  • EPS Estimate: 12 cents (Up from 6 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.05 Billion (Up from 77 cents Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 48.9x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $25.25. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Argus Research: Initiated with Hold (April 15)
  • Goldman Sachs: Neutral (Lowers Target to $20.00) (April 14)
  • Wells Fargo: Equal-Weight (Lowers Target to $18.00) (April 9)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for SoFi Technologies,, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Neutral (Score: 31.75) — The stock’s recent trend is mixed, not decisively leading or lagging.
  • Growth: Strong (Score: 92.22) — The market is pricing in above-average growth expectations versus many peers.

The Verdict: SoFi Technologies’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a growth-heavy profile, with strong growth characteristics but only moderate momentum confirmation. For longer-term bulls, the key question is whether improving momentum can persist long enough to reclaim major long-term trend levels.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because SOFI carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

SOFI Price Action: SoFi Technologies shares were down 1.65% at $18.74 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by PJ McDonnell via Shutterstock

data.title: Helen Of Troy Q4 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
data.description:
Helen Of Troy (NASDAQ:HELE) reported fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday. The transcript from the company's fourth-quarter earnings call has been provided below.

Benzinga APIs provide real-time access to earnings call transcripts and financial data. Visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ to learn more.

The full earnings call is available at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/760699127

Summary

Helen Of Troy's Q4 FY26 net sales exceeded expectations, with adjusted EPS in line, supported by strategic investments in brands and people.

The company is executing a multi-year roadmap focusing on brand momentum and operational enhancements, including supply chain visibility and digital capabilities.

For FY27, Helen Of Troy projects net sales between $1.751 billion to $1.822 billion, with a focus on restoring brand momentum and operational excellence.

Continued tariff management and dual sourcing efforts have mitigated cost impacts, with expectations to further reduce the cost of goods sold subject to China tariffs.

Management emphasizes strategic global expansion, innovation, and digital engagement to drive future growth, while maintaining a focus on debt reduction and free cash flow generation.

data.title: Live Oak Bancshares Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript
data.description:
Live Oak Bancshares (NYSE:LOB) held its first-quarter earnings conference call on Thursday. Below is the complete transcript from the call.

This transcript is brought to you by Benzinga APIs. For real-time access to our entire catalog, please visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ for a consultation.

The full earnings call is available at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/379074534

Summary

Live Oak Bancshares reported a strong Q1 2026 with an EPS of $0.60, a significant improvement over the prior year, driven by an 18% increase in revenue and controlled expenses.

The company emphasized strategic initiatives such as ramping up small dollar SBA lending and checking accounts, both of which are seen as highly accretive to earnings and crucial for their goal to be 'America's Small Business bank.'

Loan production was robust, with $1.4 billion originated across 35 industries, and the pipeline is at an all-time high, supporting confidence in future growth.

Credit trends were stable, with a slight improvement in provision expenses, and a focus on maintaining strong credit quality.

The company is leveraging technology and AI to enhance operations and customer experience, aiming to be an AI-native bank to improve efficiency and service.

Management remains optimistic about achieving long-term profitability targets of 15% ROE and 15% EPS growth, citing strong momentum in key growth areas and disciplined expense management.

Full Transcript

data.title: Transcript: Comcast Q1 2026 Earnings Conference Call
data.description:
On Thursday, Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA) discussed first-quarter financial results during its earnings call. The full transcript is provided below.

Benzinga APIs provide real-time access to earnings call transcripts and financial data. Visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ to learn more.

The full earnings call is available at https://edge.media-server.com/mmc/p/6tkch4qc/

Summary

Comcast reported an 11% increase in revenue for the first quarter, with significant contributions from the Winter Olympics and Super Bowl, and a low single-digit increase excluding these events.

The company has undergone a strategic pivot in its broadband and wireless businesses, focusing on simple pricing and premium wireless plans; it achieved record wireless net additions and improved broadband net losses year-over-year.

Comcast launched new initiatives including a mobile premium plan and leveraged major events like the Olympics and Super Bowl for marketing, contributing to high advertising sales and increased Peacock subscribers.

Parks and studios performed well, with strong growth driven by Epic Universe in Orlando and successful film releases like the Super Mario Galaxy movie.

Management is focused on leveraging its assets across NBCUniversal and maintaining competitiveness in the broadband market, while continuing to explore strategic opportunities for shareholder value.

Full Transcript

data.title: Honeywell Intl Reports Q1 2026 Results: Full Earnings Call Transcript
data.description:
On Thursday, Honeywell Intl (NASDAQ:HON) discussed first-quarter financial results during its earnings call. The full transcript is provided below.

This transcript is brought to you by Benzinga APIs. For real-time access to our entire catalog, please visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ for a consultation.

The full earnings call is available at https://event.choruscall.com/mediaframe/webcast.html?webcastid=lwGYJoGY

Summary

Honeywell Intl reported a 7% organic growth in aerospace orders and expanded its backlog to over $38 billion, with a book-to-bill ratio above 1.1, despite geopolitical challenges and supply chain constraints.

The company achieved an 11% adjusted earnings growth and a 90 basis point margin expansion to over 23%, driven by pricing discipline and productivity improvements.

Key strategic moves include the announced sale of its productivity solutions and warehouse businesses, and the planned Honeywell Aerospace spinoff expected to complete on June 29, 2026.

The company maintained its full-year organic growth outlook of 3-6%, despite temporary headwinds, with expectations for strong second-half growth supported by backlog conversion.

Management expressed confidence in future growth, emphasizing ongoing portfolio transformation, robust order growth, and strategic focus areas such as LNG projects and building automation.

Full Transcript

OPERATOR

Thank you for standing by and welcome to the Honeywell first quarter 2026 earnings conference call. At this time all participants are in a listen only mode. Thank you. Our final question this morning comes from the line of Chris Snyder with Morgan Stanley. Please proceed with your question.

Jim Currier (President and Chief Executive Officer of Honeywell Aerospace Technologies)

Thank you. Is the supply chain disruption that weighed on Arrow growth in January and February primarily a function of inability to ship because maybe some of your suppliers are struggling to keep up with the pace of demand? Or is it because your Arrow customers overbuilt inventory towards the end of last year and they were effectively destocking early in the year. Thank you.

Chris Snyder

Jim Currier (President and Chief Executive Officer of Honeywell Aerospace Technologies)

Thank you.

Chris Snyder

Yeah, I mean, what I would say is, you know, on an annualized basis, you will see us being modestly up on margin expansion quarter to quarter. You do see variability largely driven by mix across the board and mix within the mix of what we are delivering. But on a full year basis, you know we'll be modestly up for error margins. Thank you.

Vimal Kapoor (Chairman and Chief Executive Officer)

I appreciate that. Okay. As always, I would like to thank all our shareholders, our customers, and all the Honeywell future shapers around the world for the strong first quarter results you delivered. We remain confident in our path ahead, and we look forward to sharing more with everyone in the months to come. Thank you for joining us today and we hope that you have a great rest of your day.
